summary: Descriptive overview of conspiracy in substantive law: agreement, overt act, Pinkerton liability, withdrawal.
key_points: agreement is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for conspiracy are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine conspiracy in litigation.
The doctrine of conspiracy has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding conspiracy requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
